reasons: Minsitthar's trap zone makes it easier for Pharsa to land long-range artillery damage.
Pharsa can follow Minsitthar's setup from safe distance instead of standing inside the fight.

proof.summary: Minsitthar restricts enemy movement, while Pharsa punishes the trapped area from long range.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa is already close enough to follow when Minsitthar casts King’s Calling.
Minsitthar traps priority targets instead of only frontline tanks.
failureCases: Enemies spread before Pharsa can land damage.

reasons: Minsitthar keeps enemies inside a restricted area, giving Odette a better Swan Song damage window.
Odette's AoE damage becomes more valuable when enemies cannot freely dash away.

proof.summary: Minsitthar creates the restricted teamfight zone, while Odette converts that zone into sustained AoE damage.
worksBestWhen: Odette waits for Minsitthar's zone before channeling.
Minsitthar traps enemies close enough for Swan Song value.
failureCases: Odette is interrupted before her damage completes.
Enemies escape the area before Odette commits.

reasons: Aurora adds freeze control on top of Minsitthar's movement restriction.
Her AoE freeze and burst make Minsitthar's trapped area harder for enemies to survive.

proof.summary: Minsitthar restricts enemy escape tools, while Aurora adds freeze control and AoE burst into the same area.
worksBestWhen: Aurora saves her freeze and burst for enemies already trapped by Minsitthar.
Minsitthar catches enemies who rely on movement to escape.
failureCases: Aurora casts before enemies are committed inside King’s Calling.
Enemy spacing avoids being hit by both control layers.

reasons: Minsitthar's zone helps Vexana land delayed AoE damage more reliably.
Vexana adds extra control and summoned frontline pressure after Minsitthar starts the fight.

proof.summary: Minsitthar keeps enemies inside a punishable area, while Vexana follows with delayed AoE damage and control.
worksBestWhen: Vexana casts after Minsitthar confirms the enemy position.
Minsitthar traps targets near the center of Vexana's damage area.
failureCases: Vexana casts too early and enemies walk out.
Minsitthar catches targets outside Vexana's range.

reasons: Minsitthar's zone keeps enemies close enough for Luo Yi to trigger Yin-Yang Reaction more consistently.
Luo Yi can add pull, stun, and repeated area pressure when enemies are forced to stay near each other.

proof.summary: Minsitthar restricts enemy mobility, while Luo Yi punishes clustered enemies with Yin-Yang Reaction control and damage.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar traps more than one enemy or traps a target near other marked enemies.
Luo Yi prepares opposite marks before Minsitthar fully commits.
failureCases: Enemies are too spread out for Yin-Yang Reaction.
Luo Yi misses the mark timing and cannot trigger the reaction during the trap window.
